definition: Daily rotating role-play practice through sales stages (intro, discovery, offer, objections, looping) keeps scripts sharp and team aligned.
rationale: Strong coherence around daily team-based training cadence; all members emphasize same mechanism (repetition → alignment). High intra-cluster similarity (0.933). Canonical name sharpens focus on daily team element.

splits.definition: SaaS requires 7+ years to profitability with high upfront capital; existing business with margins is better foundation.
Adding software features alone does not create a SaaS company or higher valuation; business model matters.

rationale: Two distinct claims: (1) SaaS economics/capital timeline vs. (2) feature-software distinction. First two members cohere around capital/timeline axis; third is a definitional boundary claim. Split and kick the softest outlier.

canonical_name: Redeploy Founder Time: Delivery to Marketing
definition: Hire for delivery/execution first to free founder to focus 100% on promotion (sales/marketing), which is the real business lever for deal flow growth.
rationale: Tight cluster (0.937 min-sim) around founder-time allocation principle. All members emphasize same axis: delegation frees founder for higher-leverage activities. Canonical name clarifies the causal flow.

splits.definition: Maximize feedback frequency to accelerate learning; winners extract lessons from failures and iterate behavior.
Solve problems by trying many different approaches; skill deficit is the constraint, not solvability.

rationale: Cluster conflates learning-via-feedback (behavioral iteration) with learning-via-exploration (trying multiple vectors). These are orthogonal mechanisms. Split into feedback-driven iteration and exploration-driven skill development.

canonical_name: Daily Practice Builds Readiness for Opportunity
definition: Consistent daily execution of fundamentals (practice, focus, preparation) builds readiness to capitalize on opportunity when it arrives.
rationale: Cluster coherence is around daily practice as prerequisite for wins. Event Planning member is weakly connected (specific implementation detail); keep but reframe as illustrative. Renamed to emphasize the load-bearing claim (daily practice → readiness).

canonical_name: Webinar Lead Nurture via Soft Offer
definition: Free webinar offering a soft pitch (savings assessment or similar value demo) nurtures leads without hard close.
rationale: Tight, domain-specific cluster (0.985 min-sim). Both members describe same tactic. Keep as-is; low variance indicates clean concept.

definition: Secure freelance clients via Upwork by optimizing profile (title, portfolio, location, cover letter), pricing strategy, and language alignment.
rationale: All three members describe Upwork-specific tactics. Canonical name broadens beyond sign-ups to full profile optimization strategy. Good cluster coherence (0.934 min-sim).

canonical_name: Execution and Rapid Adjustment Trump Planning
definition: Perfect plans fail on contact with reality; start execution quickly, gather real information, and adjust. Commit to one path ≥18 months before pivoting.
rationale: Coherent cluster around anti-planning bias. Min-sim 0.907; all members emphasize same mechanism (planning ≠ value; execution + feedback does). Strong load-bearing claim.

definition: Minimum 1-3 posts/week on one platform keeps profile fresh; prospects check your social before responding to outreach; silent profile signals abandonment and kills trust.
rationale: Tight thematic coherence (0.923 min-sim). All members describe same mechanism: consistent content visibility → perceived legitimacy/activity. Canonical captures the load-bearing point.

definition: Price in 4 tiers with 5-10x gaps per tier, expecting ~20% takeup per step; captures entire Pareto distribution of wealth and captures majority profit.
rationale: Excellent cluster coherence around Pareto pricing model (0.911 min-sim). All members reference the 20%-80% principle and multi-tier structure. Specific, actionable, load-bearing.

canonical_name: Value = Helping Others Get What They Want

splits.definition: Value means helping others get what they want; promise meaningful help and deliver until satisfied.
When behavior change costs you little but benefits others greatly, accept it; frame gifts as helping employees to trigger reciprocity.

rationale: Two distinct mechanisms: (1) value = helping (definition) vs. (2) reciprocity alignment (tactic). First pair is definitional; second pair is tactical/incentive-based. Split for clarity.

Cluster lacks load-bearing claims. First member is anecdotal ('allowed team to rally') with no causal mechanism or conditions. Second is self-referential definition (culture = rules; codify into 3 values). Both fail NODE-QUALITY-RUBRIC A7 (definition-without-distinction) and lack falsifiability.

canonical_name: Google Business Profile Optimization for Local Visibility
definition: Optimize Google Business (My Business) listings for better visibility, reviews, and local reach.
rationale: Extremely tight cluster (0.988 min-sim); both members describe identical tactic. Renamed to modern terminology (Google Business Profile) and clarified scope (local visibility).

canonical_name: Capital Raising Channels: Affiliate, Ads, Content, Outbound

splits.definition: Reach investors via four main channels: affiliate (partner introductions), paid ads (targeted to accredited), content (attract via thought leadership), outbound (direct outreach).
Regularly create content to attract accredited investors interested in your sector; positions you as expert.

rationale: Three members (Affiliate, Paid Ads, Capital Raising Channels) form a framework of channels. Content Marketing for Investors is one instance of that framework, not a cluster. Split to avoid mixing framework with instance.

canonical_name: Geographic Monopoly Build: Fix State First, Expand Later
definition: Fix current state (increase market share/revenue within one state) before expanding to others; dominance in one market is more valuable than fragmented presence in many.
rationale: Tight cluster (0.956 min-sim) around geographic expansion strategy. Both members emphasize same principle (consolidate before expand). Renamed to clarify the load-bearing claim (monopoly in one place > expansion).
